global.title: Powerhouse Platforms

coreIdeas.contextPhrase: The execution layer — a programmable coordination system where contributors are onboarded, assigned, compensated, and recognized through transparent rule-based processes that evolve based on activity
The creation layer — instead of monolithic applications, contributors build reusable modules (document types, schemas, automation rules) published to a shared registry where usage generates revenue
The design rationale — one platform structures and stabilizes daily operations, the other opens up participation and innovation; they are complementary, not competing infrastructure
The emergent property — because any contributor can extend the system and be rewarded, Powerhouse transforms from a static platform into a living ecosystem that no single team controls

The trust mechanism — actions on the Operations Platform generate verifiable financial and reputational records, enabling disputes to be resolved and contributors to prove track records across projects
Implementation-level utility catalog for service-offering and subscription-instance editors
Core pricing engine for service-offering — computes per-selection price breakdowns across tiers
Billing calculation utilities for subscription-instance — breakdowns, overages, formatting
Operational Hub has four tiers (Essentials, Starter, Standard, Custom) structuring service levels
OH includes AI agent services for automation — where BAI/Apeiron plugs in
Crypto banking and AML compliance add-ons extend OH into regulated finance
OH subscription includes formation and ongoing ops — the full lifecycle

global.description: Two complementary platforms: Decentralized Operations (coordination engine) and Builder (reusable module creation with value sharing).
global.orientation: Powerhouse provides two complementary platforms that form the digital substrate of the SNO model. The Decentralized Operations Platform is the coordination engine — it structures daily operations through programmable, rule-based processes for compensation, budgeting, IP management, and contributor reputation, generating verifiable records that serve as shared source of truth. The Builder Platform governs creation — contributors define document types, schemas, automation rules, and interfaces as reusable modules published to a shared registry, where usage generates revenue for original authors. Together they encode governance logic, collaboration structures, and incentive mechanisms directly into software. The Operations Platform stabilizes work; the Builder Platform opens innovation. Neither is a product to sell — they are foundational infrastructure for post-corporate organizations.
